transcript:
What you're watching start as a blown-out cloud of gold and blue light actually settles, within seconds, into a single glinting trail arcing off into the dark — like a comet's tail made of static, curving and receding through space.

Here's the thing worth knowing: every one of those 2,400 points is fully alive the whole time. In updateEntities, each entity computes its complete position, velocity, and color every single frame — real physics, real trajectory — and only then does the code roll dice, checking a variable called shouldRender against a probability that starts around 38 percent and eventually drops to just 6 before settling at 15. Almost everything you're NOT seeing was still calculated. The faint smoky streaks trailing the bright core come from a separate system, addTrace, which specifically fires when something flips from hidden to visible or back — recording the moment of loss itself, not the thing lost.

So the sparse, elegant line you see isn't the artwork's full content — it's the surviving fifteen percent. The other eighty-five percent computed itself into existence and was quietly discarded, frame after frame, the whole time.
